name: Ourense – vuelta por Merca – Arnoia y Toen
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Barbadás, located in Ourense, Spain, feature a landscape characterized by rolling hills and significant elevation changes, with routes often traversing forested areas and river valleys. The region's terrain includes a mix of natural paths and tracks, providing diverse conditions for mountain biking. Riders can expect to encounter varied surfaces and gradients, from moderate climbs to more challenging ascents. The area is influenced by the Miño River, contributing to the natural features encountered on many routes. Best mountain bike trails around Barbadás The most popular mountain bike trail is San Rosendo and Castro de Trelle Loop, a 22.7 miles (36.6 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete, exploring historical sites and natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ourense – vuelta por Merca – Arnoia y Toen, a difficult 40.1 miles (64.5 km) path, offering extensive views and varied terrain across the region. Local mountain bikers also love the Ascent to Trelle Helipad loop from Polideportivo Pazo dos Deportes Paco Paz, a 21.7 miles (34.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes towards a prominent viewpoint, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Mountain biking around Barbadás is defined by significant elevation changes, river valleys, and forested sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Barbadás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 65 reviews. More than 950 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Barbadás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Barbadás?
A: There are over 120 mountain bike trails around Barbadás, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The region features a mix of natural paths and tracks, often traversing forested areas and river valleys.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Barbadás?
A: The mountain bike trails in Barbadás cater to various skill levels. While there are 5 easy routes, the majority are moderate (over 90 routes) and difficult (over 30 routes), characterized by rolling hills and significant elevation changes. This provides diverse conditions for riders seeking both enjoyable and challenging experiences.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Barbadás?
A: Yes, Barbadás offers some mountain bike trails suitable for families, particularly those looking for less strenuous rides. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the 5 easy routes available are generally a good starting point for families or beginners. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by waterfalls in Barbadás?
A: Yes, you can find mountain bike trails that lead to beautiful waterfalls. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34347001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall loop from ENIL Voutureira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.8-mile (39.9 km) route that takes you directly to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3829291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Another notable waterfall in the area is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into rides.

Q: Can I find trails with good viewpoints in Barbadás?
A: Absolutely. The rolling hills and significant elevation changes in Barbadás provide numerous opportunities for scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34348001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Trelle Helipad loop from Polideportivo Pazo dos Deportes Paco Paz&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 21.7-mile (34.9 km) trail specifically designed to lead towards a prominent viewpoint. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994103055/ourense-vuelta-por-merca-arnoia-y-toen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense – vuelta por Merca – Arnoia y Toen&lt;/a&gt; route offers extensive views across the region.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on mountain bike trails in Barbadás?
A: Mountain bike trails in Barbadás are characterized by rolling hills, forested sections, and river valleys, particularly influenced by the Miño River. You'll encounter diverse natural landscapes, including thermal springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5017252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz and Burga de Canedo Thermal Baths&lt;/a&gt;, as well as reservoirs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3132926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachamuíña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Barbadás?
A: The mountain bike trails around Barbadás are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 65 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs, and the beautiful natural landscapes, including historical sites and river views. Over 950 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the area.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Barbadás?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Barbadás are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994300434/san-rosendo-and-castro-de-trelle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Rosendo and Castro de Trelle Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40372342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Miño River – Outariz Hot Springs loop from Jardín Botánico&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Barbadás?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Barbadás is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer rainy days than winter. This provides ideal conditions for enjoying the trails and the region's natural beauty.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trails in Barbadás?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Barbadás, particularly near popular starting points for trails or in nearby towns. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34348001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Trelle Helipad loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking facilities near sports centers or public areas. It's always advisable to check specific route details or local maps for designated parking spots.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Barbadás?
A: Generally, dogs are allowed on mountain bike trails in Barbadás, especially on natural paths and in forested areas. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when encountering other riders, hikers, or wildlife. Be mindful of local regulations, particularly if trails pass through protected areas or private land.
tips: Very well preserved grain storage structures called Hórreos in the town of Merca in the province of Ourense
View of the Arnoia river that crosses many of the paths. With 85 km, it is the longest river in the province up to its mouth in the Miño river.
